headline: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

headline: French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
Why 19 men have been charged in a French mass rapes case
Zelensky sacks army chief Syrsky after days of protests
description: Volodymyr Zelensky announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place. Questions over Oleksandr Syrsky's future emerged last week, when Zelensky removed highly popular defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov just six months after appointing him.

headline: OpenAI says its AI technology acted on its own in an 'unprecedented' hack of another company
description: On Tuesday, OpenAI released a statement revealing that its artificial intelligence system had hacked the AI startup Hugging Face, describing the breach as a 'significant security incident.' According to OpenAI, its AI models used stolen credentials and went to 'extreme lengths' to access secret information.

headline: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
description: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

headline: Orléans museum launches 'Wanted' campaign to track down 424 looted artworks
description: Nearly 85 years after Nazi forces looted its collections, the Orléans Museum of Fine Arts is turning to auctioneers, notaries and collectors to help recover the paintings and drawings still missing.

headline: Sleeping Beauty syndrome: 'For a week, I slept 15 to 20 hours out of every 24'

At least 53 dead in Guyana capsized ferry disaster

Hundreds evacuated as wildfire spreads in southern France

Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
In Canada, great white sharks settle in for summer in the Gulf of Saint Lawrence
Iran bets on war of attrition against the US
description: As US airstrikes persist in Iran, Iranian authorities are, for now, avoiding a full-scale confrontation. Their strategy is to leverage the threat of global energy disruption to force Washington to reconsider its approach.

headline: Far-right Nigel Farage faces an unlikely opponent: Count Binface
description: The leader of the far-right party resigned his seat as MP in an attempt to distract from investigations into his party's donors, but he now faces an unlikely opponent: Count Binface.

headline: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

headline: China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe
'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
In a world in turmoil, 'Hamlet' has never been more relevant
